card_description: Zagreus (Greek (uncertain): "Great hunter") is a divine or mythological figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Zagreus is orphic dionysos; torn apart and reborn. PKD references Zagreus in discussions of the dismembered god reassembled. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Zagreus. Context: "Dick contrasts Gnosticism (external grace) with Neoplatonism (self-attainment), concluding that the 1974 experience was a "zygote" formation between the human "egg" and cosmic "spermatika."
